rulings.Case Title: ALISA BAKER VS. EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 5, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF ALISA BAKER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. There is a triable issue of fact regarding whether Equity Growth had notice of the TICA and whether Equity Growth's claim of exclusive possession is the result of the TICA or an implied contract. Summary adjudication as to the Cross-Complaint is moot. First Amended Cross-Complaint on file. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1341 UNION STREET TENANCY-IN-COMMON, AN VS. STEVEN SCOTT TOLLEFSRUD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 5, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT STEVEN TOLLEFSRUD , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE TRUSTEE OF THE STEVEN SCOTT TOLLEFSRUD TRUST DATED DECEMBER 22ND, 1999, (INCORRECTLY SUED AS STEVEN SCOTT TOLLESRUD AND STEVEN SCOTT TOLLESFRUD) DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, in part, and SUSTAINED, in part. The demurrers to causes of action 1, 3, 4, and 6 are OVERRULED. Plaintiffs have standing by virtue of assignment and alleged injuries to property interests. For fraud causes of action for money damages, a transfer of title is not needed for a valid assignment. The demurrer to cause of action 2 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege violations of express or implied terms with requisite specificity. The demurrer to cause of action 5 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege elder abuse and/or standing with requisite specificity. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1341 UNION STREET TENANCY-IN-COMMON, AN VS. STEVEN SCOTT TOLLEFSRUD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 5, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT STEVEN TOLLEFSRUD , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE TRUSTEE OF THE STEVEN SCOTT TOLLEFSRUD TRUST DATED DECEMBER 22ND, 1999, (INCORRECTLY SUED AS STEVEN SCOTT TOLLESRUD AND STEVEN SCOTT TOLLESFRUD) MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Paragraphs 3 and 4 of the prayers for relief, as well as paragraphs 37, 44, 52, 62, 71, 76, and 77 are stricken with leave to amend because these claims cannot be assigned without a transfer of title. Paragraphs 3 and 4 of the prayer for relief and paragraph 69 are stricken because there are no allegations that justify exemplary, double, or treble damages. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: WARREN SHUNG CHEUNG VS. DOUGLAS SYKES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Unlawful Detainer Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 5, 2025 line 5. PLAINTIFF WARREN CHEUNG Notice Of Motion To Stay Unlawful Detainer Proceedings is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice under CCP section 1005(b). The motion must be filed and served 16 court days before the hearing, plus 5 days for service by mail. Additionally, to the extent Plaintiff wishes to re-file a motion to stay the UD case, Plaintiff must file said motion in the UD case. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
